    Corporate Spend — Audit & Health Report

    BI architects, technical leads, and governance teams · generated TIMESTAMP

    Findings

    This model scores 77/100 overall (Good), with medium structural complexity across 11 tables, 15 measures, and 8 relationships. Unused assets is the area holding the score back (70/100), while performance is the strongest area (88/100). 19 DAX findings, 5 failed best-practice checks, 9 performance risk signals, and 11 governance findings were identified. The top priority is: Some data sources use a hardcoded local file path. (High) — Replace the literal path with a Power Query parameter, or move the source behind a gateway/shared location. ```m // Replace each hardcoded path with a Power Query parameter // was: Source = File.Contents("C:\Users\mad\Dropbox\Work\Obvience\IP\IT\Data\Clean\Data.xlsx") let SourcePath = #"Path" /* Power Query parameter, e.g. bound to a SharePoint/gateway location */ in Source = File.Contents(SourcePath) ```

    4. Model Best Practices

    RuleCheckResultDetail
    PBIC-MOD-007Star schemaFailModel shape detected as a multi-fact (galaxy) schema with 2 fact tables.
    PBIC-MOD-008Fact/dimension separationPass2 fact tables, 6 dimension tables.
    PBIC-NAM-001Table naming conventionsPassNo generic/default table names detected.
    PBIC-DOC-001Display folder usageFail0/15 measures use a display folder.
    PBIC-DOC-002Description coverageFail0/40 measures and visible columns have a description.
    PBIC-MOD-005Dedicated date tablePass'Date' looks like the model's date table.
    PBIC-MOD-006Hidden technical/key columnsFail4/10 ID/key-like columns are hidden.
    PBIC-MOD-010Unused calculated columnsFail10 calculated columns not referenced by any visual: DateTableTemplate_5db4a9d0-37ae-4eb8-b985-081b6a9bccc6[Year], DateTableTemplate_5db4a9d0-37ae-4eb8-b985-081b6a9bccc6[MonthNo], DateTableTemplate_5db4a9d0-37ae-4eb8-b985-081b6a9bccc6[QuarterNo], DateTableTemplate_5db4a9d0-37ae-4eb8-b985-081b6a9bccc6[Quarter], DateTableTemplate_5db4a9d0-37ae-4eb8-b985-081b6a9bccc6[Day], LocalDateTable_c659a3fc-ad91-4f80-b458-6b22a6d14a66[Year], LocalDateTable_c659a3fc-ad91-4f80-b458-6b22a6d14a66[MonthNo], LocalDateTable_c659a3fc-ad91-4f80-b458-6b22a6d14a66[QuarterNo], LocalDateTable_c659a3fc-ad91-4f80-b458-6b22a6d14a66[Quarter], LocalDateTable_c659a3fc-ad91-4f80-b458-6b22a6d14a66[Day].
    PBIC-MOD-009Inactive relationshipsPassNo inactive relationships.
    PBIC-MOD-001Bidirectional cross-filteringPassNo bidirectional relationships.
    PBIC-MOD-002Many-to-many relationshipsPassNo many-to-many relationships.
    PBIC-MOD-014Many-to-many relationship without a bridge tablePassEvery many-to-many relationship routes through a bridge table, or none exist.
    PBIC-MOD-015Bidirectional relationship filtering a fact tablePassNo bidirectional relationships touch a fact table.
    PBIC-MOD-003No circular relationship pathsPassNo circular relationship paths detected.
    PBIC-MOD-004No disconnected fact/dimension tablesPassEvery fact/dimension table participates in at least one relationship.
    PBIC-NAM-002No development leftovers in production modelPassNo table/column names match common development-leftover patterns.
    PBIC-MOD-012Summarize-by setting on key columnsPassKey/ID columns have SummarizeBy set to None.
    PBIC-MOD-013Snowflake dimension depth limitPassAll dimension tables are within recommended chain depth of 2.
    PBIC-NAM-004Column naming conventions (typos)PassNo typos detected in column names.

    5. Performance Risks

    All performance risks below are heuristics inferred from metadata only — no row-level data is ever extracted, so nothing here reflects a measured runtime.

    RuleKindObjectTableSeverityDetail
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalCost Element IDFactLow'Cost Element ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalCountry/Region IDFactLow'Country/Region ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalBusiness Area IDFactLow'Business Area ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalIT Sub Area IDFactLow'IT Sub Area ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalScenario IDFactLow'Scenario ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalIT Sub Area IDIT AreaLow'IT Sub Area ID' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-003Large Text ColumnScenarioDescriptionScenarioLow'ScenarioDescription' looks like a free-text column (name pattern + string type) — heuristic signal only, not measured against actual text length.
    PBIC-PERF-004Visual DensityPlan Variance Analysis—Low19 visuals on one page — more visuals means more concurrent queries on every filter change.
    PBIC-PERF-007Auto DatetimeAuto Date/Time—MediumAuto date/time is enabled in this model — 2 hidden local tables with 14 columns total are created for date columns, increasing file size. These hidden tables' own columns are its own category, excluded from the Unused Assets report below rather than inflating it — disabling Auto Date/Time (Options > Data Load) removes them entirely.

    6. Governance

    RuleAreaSeverityDetail
    PBIC-GOV-011Rls AbsentMediumNo row-level security roles are defined in this model.

    PBIC-GOV-003OwnershipMediumNo owner or data classification was specified when this documentation was generated.

    PBIC-GOV-006Unused PagesLowReport page 'Tooltip' is hidden and has no drill-through targets configured (potentially unused leftover).

    PBIC-GOV-009Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Fact' and 'Business Area' references a hidden column.

    7. Unused Assets

    Asset TypeCountItems
    Measures1Actual/Plan
    Columns18Cost Element[Cost element name], Cost Element[Cost Element Group], Cost Element[Cost Element Sub Group], Date[Year], Department[VP], Department[Department], Fact[Date], Fact[Value], Fact[Department], Fact[Cost Element ID], Fact[Country/Region ID], Fact[Business Area ID], Fact[IT Sub Area ID], Fact[Scenario ID], IT Area[IT Sub Area ID], Range[www.obvience.com], Scenario[Scenario], Scenario[ScenarioDescription]
    Tables1Range
    Calculated columns0—
    Report pages1Tooltip

    Hierarchies and calculation groups are documented in the technical document's Data Model section; this unused-asset audit covers tables, columns, and measures.

    10 additional otherwise-unused items belonging to Power BI's auto-generated Auto Date/Time tables are excluded from the counts above — see PBIC-PERF-007 in Performance Risks.

    8. Recommendations

    High Some data sources use a hardcoded local file path.PBIC-GOV-010

    Why it matters: Refresh fails as soon as the report runs on any machine or service other than the author's.

    Suggested fix: Replace the literal path with a Power Query parameter, or move the source behind a gateway/shared location.

    // Replace each hardcoded path with a Power Query parameter
// was: Source = File.Contents("C:\Users\mad\Dropbox\Work\Obvience\IP\IT\Data\Clean\Data.xlsx")
let
    SourcePath = #"Path" /* Power Query parameter, e.g. bound to a SharePoint/gateway location */
in
    Source = File.Contents(SourcePath)

    Expected benefit: Refresh works after deployment to the Power BI Service.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium The model does not follow a star schema.PBIC-MOD-007

    Why it matters: Non-star shapes (snowflake, galaxy, flat) typically perform worse and are harder to reason about for report authors.

    Suggested fix: Where practical, re-model dimensions to relate directly to fact tables. Fact tables involved: 'Date', 'Fact'.

    Expected benefit: Simpler, faster, more predictable filter propagation.

    Estimated effort: High

    Medium Many measures/columns have no description.PBIC-DOC-002

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions mean new team members must reverse-engineer intent from DAX and column names alone.

    Suggested fix: Add descriptions to the most-used measures and columns first. Undescribed measures: Amount, Actual, LE1, LE2, LE3, Plan, Var LE1, Var LE2 (+7 more). Undescribed columns: Business Area[Business Area], Cost Element[Cost element name], Cost Element[Cost Element Group], Cost Element[Cost Element Sub Group], Country Region[Sales Region], Country Region[Country/Region], Date[Year], Date[Period] (+17 more).

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and self-documenting metadata in Power BI Desktop tooltips.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium No row-level security is configured for this report.PBIC-GOV-011

    Why it matters: Every viewer of this report can see every row, including any data intended for a narrower audience.

    Suggested fix: Decide whether this data should be restricted by user. If it should, define RLS roles and assign members; if not, record that unrestricted access is intended.

    Expected benefit: A deliberate, documented decision about who can see which rows.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Medium Description coverage across measures and columns is low.PBIC-GOV-002

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions increase onboarding time and the risk of misinterpreting a field's meaning.

    Suggested fix: Prioritize descriptions for the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misunderstandings.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium No owner or data classification was specified for this report.PBIC-GOV-003

    Why it matters: Without a named owner, there's no clear point of contact for questions, incidents, or change requests.

    Suggested fix: Assign a business owner and a data classification (e.g. Internal, Confidential) in the documentation metadata.

    Expected benefit: Clear accountability and appropriate handling of the data.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some measures have no description.PBIC-DAX-003

    Why it matters: Without a description, business meaning has to be reverse-engineered from the DAX every time.

    Suggested fix: Add a one-sentence description to each measure explaining what it calculates and why. Affected measures: Amount, Actual, LE1, LE2, LE3, Plan, Var LE1, Var LE2, Var LE3, Var Plan (+5 more).

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misinterpretations of what a measure means.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Deep measure dependency chains were found.PBIC-DAX-010

    Why it matters: Chains of measures referencing other measures deeper than 3 levels are fragile and hard to refactor/debug.

    Suggested fix: Simplify the dependency chain or consolidate intermediate calculations.

    Expected benefit: More maintainable measures that are easier to debug.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Few measures are organized into display folders.PBIC-DOC-001

    Why it matters: Without display folders, the field list becomes a long, unorganized list as the model grows.

    Suggested fix: Group related measures into display folders (e.g. 'Sales', 'Time Intelligence').

    Expected benefit: A field list that's easier to navigate for report authors.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some ID/key-style columns are visible to report authors.PBIC-MOD-006

    Why it matters: Visible technical join keys clutter the field list and can be mis-used in visuals by mistake.

    Suggested fix: Hide columns that exist only to support relationships.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to hide key/ID columns
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Cost Element ID"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Country/Region ID"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Business Area ID"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["IT Sub Area ID"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Scenario ID"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["IT Area"].Columns["IT Sub Area ID"].IsHidden = true;

    Expected benefit: A cleaner field list focused on business-relevant fields.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some calculated columns are never used in any report visual.PBIC-MOD-010

    Why it matters: Unused calculated columns still consume model size and refresh time for no analytical benefit.

    Suggested fix: Remove calculated columns that are not referenced anywhere, or confirm they are needed for row-level security/relationships.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and faster refreshes.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some visible columns look like high-cardinality identifiers.PBIC-PERF-002

    Why it matters: High-cardinality columns compress poorly and can slow down the model if used in visuals or relationships.

    Suggested fix: Hide the column if it's a technical key, or confirm its cardinality is acceptable for the intended use.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and better query performance, if confirmed unnecessary.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some columns look like free-text fields.PBIC-PERF-003

    Why it matters: Long free-text columns are expensive to store and rarely useful for aggregation or filtering.

    Suggested fix: Confirm the column is needed in the model, or exclude it from the import if it's not used in any visual.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some report pages have a large number of visuals.PBIC-PERF-004

    Why it matters: Every visual on a page issues its own query on load and on every filter change — more visuals means more concurrent load.

    Suggested fix: Split dense pages into multiple focused pages, or use bookmarks to progressively reveal detail.

    Expected benefit: Faster page load and filter response times.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Low 21 unused assets were found (measures, columns, tables, calculated columns, or hidden non-drillthrough pages).

    Why it matters: Unused assets add to model size, refresh time, and cognitive load for anyone maintaining the report.

    Suggested fix: Remove unused assets, or confirm they're intentionally kept for a documented reason (e.g. future use, RLS support).

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to delete unused assets
Model.Tables["Cost Element"].Columns["Cost element name"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Cost Element"].Columns["Cost Element Group"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Cost Element"].Columns["Cost Element Sub Group"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Date"].Columns["Year"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Department"].Columns["VP"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Department"].Columns["Department"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Date"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Value"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Department"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Cost Element ID"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Country/Region ID"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Business Area ID"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["IT Sub Area ID"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Fact"].Columns["Scenario ID"].Delete();
Model.Tables["IT Area"].Columns["IT Sub Area ID"].Delete();
// ... and more

    Expected benefit: A leaner, easier-to-maintain model.

    Estimated effort: Low
